mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which expands corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlled for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods where INDPRO increases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during inflationary supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, boosting consumer income and corporate profit forecasts to generate positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when adjusted for seasonal employment patterns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth over a 5-year sample, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 65% of periods where INDPRO falls > 0.5%, or the signal fails to predict short-term direction with p < 0.05.
